what_it_does: Implements file-discovered registries for reusable `skill://<id>` and `context://<id>` entries: it loads `skills/<id>.py` and `contexts/<id>.py` modules, validates their declared `SKILL`/`CONTEXT` dicts, and exposes `SkillRegistry`/`ContextRegistry` objects that can discover, register, and read back the declared `content`.
observations: Uses `importlib.util` to load arbitrary `.py` files from configured directories at runtime.
Both skill and context entries share a single row schema (`id`, `content`, optional `label`/`description`) represented by the `Entry` dataclass.
Validation is strict and fail-loud: missing/non-string `id`, `id` not matching filename, unknown fields, or missing/empty `content` all raise.
The lookup method is deliberately named `read`, not `resolve`, to avoid a forbidden dotted-resolve verb under a stated source-invariant.
`_BaseEntryRegistry` centralizes all behavior; `SkillRegistry` and `ContextRegistry` only set `KIND` and `ATTR`.
Provides dict-like accessors (`__getitem__`, `__contains__`, `__iter__`, `get`) in addition to `read`.
`discover` only adds entries; `rediscover` clears then rediscovers so removed files unregister.
standouts: Strict schema validation including `id == filename` and rejection of unknown fields.
Deliberate naming of `read` to respect a governance invariant about dotted `resolve`.
Single base registry reused for two vocabularies, mirroring the stated role/node registry pattern.
conventions: File-discovered registry pattern instead of hardcoded lists.
Fail-loud validation for malformed declared entries.
Module-level declaration dict (`SKILL`/`CONTEXT`) self-registers by file name.
Skips files starting with `_` or lacking the declared attribute, mirroring other registries.
Uses dataclass for the typed registry row and properties for optional fields.
concerns: Dynamic execution of arbitrary `.py` files from configured directories is a trust boundary; malicious or accidental code in `skills/`/`contexts/` runs during discovery.
Duplicate ids across multiple discovery directories silently overwrite each other (last-wins).
`read` raises `ValueError` for unknown ids rather than a more conventional `KeyError`.
No metadata returned with the content (e.g., source path, mtime, hash) — only the string is exposed.
notes: This is the substrate that turns `skills/<id>.py` and `contexts/<id>.py` files into addressable `skill://` and `context://` vocabulary entries, with loud validation guarding registry integrity.
questions: Where are the `skills/` and `contexts/` directory paths configured for discovery?
How does `runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address` dispatch `skill://` and `context://` schemes to these registries?
Is there any sandboxing or signature checking for the dynamically loaded skill/context modules?
Does `content` support any parameterization/templating, or is it always returned verbatim?
How is the `AGENTS.md` drift-home acceptance test wired to this module in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a file-discovered registry seam for skills and contexts with the same shape and validation as role/node registries.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish skills and contexts as first-class, addressable registry entries rather than inline literals, and to enforce that any declared entry is well-formed and discoverable by its file name.
novelty: Treats reusable instruction and context blobs as registry rows using the same discovery/validation machinery as node/role types, and intentionally renames the resolution operation to `read` to satisfy a source-invariant.
contribution: Provides `SkillRegistry`, `ContextRegistry`, and the `Entry` schema/validation logic that a fused system would keep to discover, validate, and read `skills/<id>.py` and `contexts/<id>.py` files as addressable vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: File-discovered registry implementation for reusable skill and context entries, validating module-level SKILL/CONTEXT declarations and returning their content on read.
intention_for_embedding: To extend the address resolver with skill:// and context:// vocabularies using the same registry pattern as roles and node types.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing one discovered skill or context row.
symbols.description: `Entry` stores the validated `id`, the resolvable `content` string, the original declared spec dict, and the kind (`skill` or `context`). It exposes `label` and `description` properties that fall back to the id when no label is declared.

symbols.does: Property returning the entry's declared label or its id.
symbols.description: Returns `spec.get('label')` if present, otherwise the entry `id`, giving every row a human-readable name.

symbols.does: Property returning the optional one-line description.
symbols.description: Returns the declared `description` field from the spec, or `None` if none was provided.

symbols.does: Dynamically loads a Python file by path and returns its module name and module object.
symbols.description: Uses `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` and `exec_module` to load an arbitrary `.py` file so its module-level `SKILL`/`CONTEXT` dict can be inspected.

symbols.does: Validates a declared dict and constructs an `Entry`, raising on any malformed declaration.
symbols.description: Checks that the declaration is a dict, has a string `id` matching the filename, contains only known schema fields, and has a non-empty string `content`; raises `TypeError` or `ValueError` otherwise.

symbols.does: Abstract base class implementing file-discovered registry behavior shared by skills and contexts.
symbols.description: Provides discovery, rediscovery, registration, `read`, and dict-like access. Subclasses only set `KIND` and `ATTR` to specialize for skills or contexts.
Creates the internal `entries: dict[str, Entry]` mapping, ready for discovery or manual registration.

symbols.does: Scans directories for non-underscore `.py` files and registers those declaring the expected attribute.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted directory listings, loads each candidate module, and if the module declares the configured attribute (`SKILL`/`CONTEXT`), validates and registers it.

symbols.does: Clears the registry and re-runs discovery.
symbols.description: Ensures removed files unregister and changed files re-register by clearing `entries` before calling `discover`.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a declaration under the given module name.
symbols.description: Wraps `_build_entry` and assigns the resulting `Entry` into `self.entries[name]`.

symbols.does: Returns the `content` string for a known entry id, raising otherwise.
symbols.description: Looks up the entry by id and returns its `content`; deliberately named `read` rather than `resolve` and raises `ValueError` for unknown ids to enforce registry-is-truth.

symbols.does: Dict-like access returning the `Entry` object for an id.
symbols.description: Allows `reg[id]` to retrieve the full `Entry`, not just its content.

symbols.does: Dict-like membership test for an entry id.
symbols.description: Returns whether the id is present in the registry's entries.
Yields the keys of the internal `entries` dict, supporting `for id in reg`.

symbols.does: Dict-like optional lookup returning an `Entry` or a default.
symbols.description: Mirrors `dict.get`, returning the `Entry` for the id or the supplied default if absent.

symbols.does: Concrete registry for `skills/<id>.py` files declaring a `SKILL` dict.
symbols.description: Sets `KIND='skill'` and `ATTR='SKILL'` so that discovery loads reusable instruction entries addressable as `skill://<id>`.

symbols.does: Concrete registry for `contexts/<id>.py` files declaring a `CONTEXT` dict.
symbols.description: Sets `KIND='context'` and `ATTR='CONTEXT'` so that discovery loads reusable context blobs addressable as `context://<id>`.
